title: psacalium laxiflorum
intro: Psacalium laxiflorum, also known as Yerba Santa, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Psacalium laxiflorum include soothing respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, and aiding in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Yerba Santa can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall well-being and immunity.
